ocr.text: The table presents a comprehensive overview of various drugs, their associated diseases, relevant DNA mutations, and actions.
For Chronic myelogenous leukemia, the drugs Imatinib, Dasatinib, Nilotinib, and Bosutinib target the BCR-ABL1 fusion,
indicating their use in therapy. Ponatinib is also indicated for Chronic myelogenous leukemia but is specifically noted for T315I
mutations.
Drug  Disease  DNA mutation  Action
Imatinib, Dasatinib, Nilotinib, Bosutinib  Chronic myelogenous leukemia  BCR-ABL1 fusion  Indication for therapy
Ponatinib  Chronic myelogenous leukemia  BCR-ABL1 fusion  Only indicated for T315I mutations
T315I resistance mutation
Erlotinib, Afatinib  Lung adenocarcinoma  EGFR  Indication for therapy
Exon 19 deletions
L858R
Vemurafenib, Dabrafenib  Melanoma  BRAF V600E  Indication for therapy
Tramatenib  Melanoma  BRAF V600E/K  Indication for therapy
Crizotinib  Lung cancer  ALK gene fusions  Indication for therapy
Cetuximab  Colon cancer  KRAS codon 12, 13  Contraindication to therapy
Olaparib  Ovarian cancer  BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations  Indication for therapy
The T315I resistance mutation itself is acknowledged but lacks a specific drug association. In the context of Lung
adenocarcinoma, Erlotinib and Afatinib are indicated for therapy due to the presence of EGFR mutations, including Exon 19
deletions and the L858R mutation. For Melanoma, Vemurafenib and Dabrafenib are indicated for therapy targeting the BRAF
V600E mutation, while Tramatenib is also indicated for the BRAF V600E/K mutation. Crizotinib is noted for its therapeutic
indication in Lung cancer, targeting ALK gene fusions. In Colon cancer, Cetuximab is listed as a contraindication to therapy for
KRAS codon 12 and 13 mutations. Lastly, Olaparib is indicated for therapy in Ovarian cancer, specifically for patients with
BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations. This detailed table encapsulates essential information regarding drug applications based on
specific diseases and genetic mutations, highlighting the importance of precision in cancer treatment protocols.
